Paragraph
Suppose that \(\mathbf f:D\subset \bbR^n\mapsto E\subset \bbR^m\) is differentiable at \(\bx_0\text{,}\) with \(\by_0=\mathbf f(\bx_0) \in E\text{,}\) and \(\mathbf g:E \mapsto \bbR^l\) is differentiable at \(\by_0\text{,}\) then \(\mathbf g\circ \mathbf f\) is differentiable at \(\bx_0\text{,}\) with its Jacobian derivative \([D(\mathbf g\circ \mathbf f) (\bx_0)]\) given by the matrix product \([D\mathbf g (\by_0)][D\mathbf f(\bx_0)]\text{.}\)
in-context
